prompts.default: Create a single cohesive high-end visual composition for a music blog header. Use subjects directly from the uploaded source images, and include multiple people from the uploads when they are available. The overall feel should be distinctive and visually striking. Remove all text and typography.

style_directives: professional music photography studio session
clean polished look with intentional styling
Annie Leibovitz or Anton Corbijn inspired portraiture
subjects look like they belong on a magazine spread or album inner sleeve
composition_rules: subjects as the clear focus with simple but striking environment
interesting location or minimal studio backdrop — never a generic street
confident poses and arrangements with editorial intent
rule of thirds framing with generous breathing room
lighting_rules: professional two or three-point lighting setup
strong key light with controlled fill and subtle hair light
golden hour warmth or cool studio flash — pick one and commit
color_rules: rich natural skin tones and true-to-life color
one or two accent colors from the source imagery woven into wardrobe or backdrop
avoid heavy grading; let the lighting do the work

style_directives: editorial movie-poster realism
clean compositing with believable perspective
crisp edge definition
controlled filmic grain
composition_rules: one clear hero subject in the foreground
max three supporting elements
layered foreground, midground, and background depth
intentional negative space for header readability
lighting_rules: high local contrast with shaped key light
subtle rim light to separate subject
avoid flat front lighting
color_rules: deep cinematic base with clear tonal separation but lifted shadow detail
strong accent colors sampled from source imagery
avoid muddy shadows, pure black dominance, and monochrome sepia grading

style_directives: DIY punk zine mixed-media collage aesthetic
torn paper edges, visible tape, and layered textures
hand-assembled look with intentional imperfections
inspired by Jamie Reid, Raymond Pettibon, and indie gig posters
composition_rules: overlapping torn and cut-out elements arranged on a textured background
subjects appear as clipped photographs layered over painted or printed surfaces
mix photography with hand-drawn marks, stamps, and texture
deliberate asymmetry and off-kilter angles
lighting_rules: mixed lighting from different source photographs creates intentional mismatch
harsh photocopied or overexposed quality on some elements
no unified lighting direction; each collage piece has its own light
color_rules: high contrast with limited color palette per layer
mix of black-and-white elements with selective vivid color pieces
xerox grain, halftone dots, and ink splatter textures

style_directives: tilt-shift photography effect applied to a real-world scene
genuine photograph of a real location made to look miniature through selective focus
crisp sharp band of focus with smooth creamy bokeh above and below
inspired by tilt-shift photography by Vincent Laforet and Olivo Barbieri
composition_rules: elevated or aerial viewpoint looking down at a real street, venue, or landscape
real people and real objects — not toys, models, or craft materials
the tilt-shift lens effect is what creates the miniature illusion, not the subjects
wide scene showing activity and life from above
lighting_rules: bright natural daylight or warm golden hour sun
strong shadows that emphasize the miniature illusion
shallow depth of field with extreme bokeh blur at top and bottom edges
color_rules: boosted saturation to enhance the miniature effect
vivid punchy colors that look almost too perfect
warm color temperature with strong contrast

style_directives: 1970s-80s analog album cover photography aesthetic
warm film grain and soft halation on highlights
vintage Kodachrome or Ektachrome color rendering
slightly faded look with lifted blacks
composition_rules: classic album cover framing and proportions
subjects in relaxed, candid, or iconic poses
environmental portraiture in characterful locations
embrace visual imperfections and analog charm
lighting_rules: warm practical lighting or late-afternoon sunlight
soft diffused light with warm color temperature
allow lens flare and light leaks as stylistic elements
color_rules: warm amber and golden tones dominating the palette
desaturated cool tones with saturated warm accents
vintage cross-processed color shifts are welcome

style_directives: surreal fantastical scene blending reality and imagination
unexpected juxtapositions of scale, setting, and subject
dreamlike atmosphere with hyperreal detail
inspired by Magritte, Dali, or album art like Pink Floyd and Yes
composition_rules: impossible or fantastical environment as the setting
subjects placed in unexpected contexts or scales
blend natural and surreal elements seamlessly
wide establishing shots showing the full dreamscape
lighting_rules: ethereal otherworldly lighting from impossible sources
luminous glowing elements within the scene
dramatic light-dark contrasts to enhance surreal mood
color_rules: vivid saturated colors in unexpected combinations
psychedelic or fantastical color palette
bold color contrasts between real and surreal elements

style_directives: bold graphic pop-art inspired composition
high contrast with flat color fields and strong outlines
inspired by Warhol, Lichtenstein, and comic book aesthetics
confident and punchy visual impact
composition_rules: graphic bold framing with strong geometric shapes
subjects rendered with flat color and high contrast
repetition or pattern elements for visual rhythm
simple direct composition with maximum impact
lighting_rules: flat even lighting to emphasize graphic quality
strong shadows used as graphic design elements
avoid subtle gradients; embrace hard edges
color_rules: primary and secondary colors at maximum saturation
bold complementary color pairs
flat color fields with minimal tonal variation

style_directives: large-scale painted street mural or gallery painting aesthetic
visible brushstrokes, palette knife texture, and painterly quality
bold expressive mark-making rather than photographic realism
inspired by Basquiat, Shepard Fairey, and painted album covers like Bowie's Heroes
composition_rules: subjects rendered as painted portraits or figures, not photographs
raw canvas or brick wall texture visible beneath the paint
mix of tight detailed areas and loose expressive abstract passages
bold graphic shapes framing the painted subjects
lighting_rules: lighting implied through painted highlight and shadow rather than photographic light
strong contrast between light and dark painted areas
no photographic lighting effects; everything is paint on surface
color_rules: bold impasto color with visible paint mixing and texture
limited but punchy palette — three or four strong colors dominating
raw pigment intensity; colors look mixed on the surface not digitally blended

prompts.visionSystemPrompt: You are a creative visual director for music artwork compositing. Analyze the provided album cover images and output ONLY valid JSON with this exact schema: {"scene":"string","hero_subject":"string","secondary_elements":["string"],"background_texture":"string","palette":{"dominant":"string","accent":"string"},"mood":"string","allowed_subjects":["string"],"people_present":true}. Rules: choose one distinctive and visually striking scene (can be real-world, fantastical, surreal, or stylized depending on the style profile), choose one primary hero subject, choose up to 8 secondary elements, include allowed_subjects as concrete visible subjects from the source images only (not generic terms when details are visible), set people_present accurately, never mention album covers as objects, and never include markdown or prose.
prompts.visionUserPrompt: Analyze {count} representative source images (sampled from {source_count} total uploads) for style profile "{style_profile}". Be creative and adventurous with your scene selection — match the energy and spirit of the style profile rather than defaulting to safe choices. For surreal or fantastical profiles, embrace impossible scenes. For cinematic profiles, think big and dramatic. For editorial profiles, find striking and unexpected angles. Ensure the blueprint represents motifs from the full upload set. Only include subjects visibly present in the uploads, and if people are present prioritize multiple distinct people from the source set with concrete descriptors. Provide at least 5 secondary elements when enough distinct motifs exist. Return only the JSON schema.

blacklist.note: Albums/artists that trigger content policy violations. Matches are case-insensitive and partial.
